name: Human idea 7 Register reuse: keep the outer 64x384 hot path, but serialise the inner live set into 2x192 micro-panels

summary.name: Human idea 7 Register reuse: keep the outer 64x384 hot path, but serialise the inner live set into 2x192 micro-panels
summary.hypothesis: The current correct 64x384 PTX path is no longer barrier- or mio-limited: barrier is only 6.58%, mio throttle is 0.33%, DRAM is 17.39%, but tensor active is still only 38.58% and warps active only 16.59% with launch__occupancy_limit_registers = 1. That is a classic register-wall signature. The highest-upside next move is therefore not another feed tweak first; it is to keep the outer CTA count advantage of 64x384 while cutting the live accumulator and B-fragment set roughly in half by issuing two serial 192-column subpanels inside the same fixed peeled kernel. This preserves the proven macro shape while targeting the actual limiter that remains on the correct branch.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Register pressure and resulting latency-hiding loss on the stable 64x384 PTX hot path.
summary.risk: Moderate. This stays on the correct branch, but it touches the hot PTX control path and can easily give back the CTA-count win if the extra serialisation adds too much steady-state overhead.
summary.stop_condition: Keep this family only if correctness stays PASS and registers/active-warps move in the right direction. Reject it if runtime regresses materially without a clear occupancy improvement.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
